body:
## Summary
- Add explicit `/setup apply` routing with conservative issue-only authorization and implicit-apply rejection.
- Add a typed setup-apply CLI that validates setup issue intent, creates/updates only allowlisted repo variables, and updates one marked audit comment.
- Add `AGENT_ASSIGNMENT_ENABLED` and Project owner/title setup context, with docs and focused regression coverage.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/setup-apply.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/setup-apply-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/assign-agent-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/resolve-dispatch-cli.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #148

comments.body:
Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| implement | review | PR #149 | 3 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777939277737 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNDk2NjMxNTA6aW1wbGVtZW50OjE0ODpyZXZpZXc6MTQ5OjM -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 95 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | `/setup apply` uses a stable marker and updates an existing audit comment before creating a new one in [`setup-apply.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/149/files#diff-083b6f80f733d7ce615e46fe84f69014099b3fae7e4f4c35897664b32dc1cb45). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The PR parses structured setup issue sections into typed intent fields and adds separate `project_owner` / `project_title` workflow inputs instead of overloading URL/ID strings. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | Dry-run and real apply use the same parsed intent, allowlisted plan, validation, and audit formatter; tests cover dry-run skipping writes and invalid confirmation blocking writes. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #149 implements child #148 under parent #126 and preserves the staged boundary from child #146: plan route first, deterministic apply next, no Project creation/linking yet. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to setup-apply routing, setup variable application, assignment toggle plumbing, project planning context, docs, workflows, and focused tests required by #148. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation keeps workflow glue thin and puts GitHub/API behavior in typed `.agent/src` helpers/CLI, reusing existing `gh` and `postIssueComment` helpers. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 5/7 | Setup route, access policy, project-planning, quick-start, configuration, and deployment docs were updated; however, the deployment permission docs appear to describe repository variable updates as **Actions** permission rather than the GitHub App/PAT **Variables** write permission needed by `gh variable set`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Apply validation failures and `gh variable set` failures produce blocked audit output, attempt to post/update the setup audit comment, and exit nonzero in [`setup-apply.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/149/files#diff-2ac8c74ad63f8a7013267d5b77a7c1702f76b0bf26c339a59c60454abea00947). |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | The setup audit comment uses a short status line plus a compact variable before/after/result table and moves boundary notes into a short notes list. |

## Notes

- Strongest rubric fit: the PR makes `/setup apply` explicit, issue-only, access-gated, deterministic, allowlisted, and auditable, matching the parent #126 staged plan and child #148 scope.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent run build` passed, then the focused node test command from the PR passed with `122` tests.
- Smallest useful follow-up: update setup/app deployment docs to mention the required **Variables: write** permission for repository variable updates.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Keep docs in sync: deployment docs currently say `/setup apply` repository variable updates need **Actions: read and write**, but the implementation calls `gh variable set`, which requires repository variables write capability for GitHub App/fine-grained PAT setups. This could make self-hosted setup fail even when users follow the documented permission list.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #149 implements issue #148, a staged child under parent #126 for deterministic `/setup apply`.
The goal is to add an explicit, issue-only, privileged setup route that applies only allowlisted repository variables after setup issue confirmation.
The PR keeps the intended boundary: it does not create/link GitHub Projects, update Project fields, sync Project items, or let the model run arbitrary mutation commands.
It also updates routing, access policy docs, setup/project-management docs, workflow wiring, and focused tests.

## Review
The high-level architecture is right, but two privilege/state-reporting issues need branch changes before merge.
Reviewer severity differed: Claude and rubrics were largely positive, while Codex flagged blocking auth and audit correctness issues; the concrete code findings below should be resolved.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Setup-apply access override can widen mutation permissions | BLOCKING | [`setup-apply`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db/.agent/src/access-policy.ts#L116) inherits `route_overrides.setup`, so a repo that widened the plan-only route also grants variable-write access. |
| Setup apply audits can misreport failed writes | BLOCKING | The audit formatter reports planned `created`/`updated` results even when validation blocks writes or when variable application fails partway through. |
| Malformed setup form values default into mutations | WARNING | Unknown or missing setup form fields default to `dry-run`, `false`, or `none`, which can create a mutation plan instead of failing closed. |
| Audit comment lookup is not paginated | WARNING | The marker lookup reads only the default issue-comments page, so long setup threads can miss an existing audit comment and post duplicates. |
| Deployment docs list the wrong permission for variable writes | WARNING | The docs attribute `/setup apply` variable updates to Actions write access, but repo variable writes need Variables write permission for GitHub App/fine-grained PAT setups. |

## Progress
- The PR already implements explicit `/setup apply` routing, issue-only dispatch, a deterministic CLI, an allowlist, an audit marker, docs, and focused tests.
- Reviewers reported the `.agent` build and focused test suite passing on PR head `b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db`.
- Inline comments were posted for the four code-local findings; the docs permission issue was already clearly covered in the top-level rubrics review.
- Project creation/linking and Project field sync are intentionally out of scope for this child.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Setup-apply access override can widen mutation permissions</summary>

**Cause:** [`getAllowedAssociationsForRoute`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db/.agent/src/access-policy.ts#L116) falls back from `setup-apply` to `route_overrides.setup`.

**Candidate solutions:** Require `route_overrides.setup-apply` explicitly for the mutating route; otherwise use the conservative built-in `setup-apply` default. Update tests and docs to match.

**Comments:** Rubrics considered access generally sound, but this inheritance changes the meaning of an existing plan-route override.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Setup apply audits can misreport failed writes</summary>

**Cause:** [`formatSetupApplyAudit`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db/.agent/src/setup-apply.ts#L448) renders planned actions, and the CLI catch path reuses that plan after a possible partial write.

**Candidate solutions:** Carry actual per-variable outcomes through apply, render validation-blocked rows as not applied, and handle mid-loop failures with applied/failed/not-attempted or verified-after-failure states.

**Comments:** This matters because the audit comment is the user-visible record of privileged repository variable mutation.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Malformed setup form values default into mutations</summary>

**Cause:** [`parseProjectManagementMode`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db/.agent/src/setup-apply.ts#L113) defaults unknown input to `dry-run`; assignment and Project choice parsing also silently default.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate required setup issue sections and accepted option values explicitly, then fail with a clear audit error on missing or unknown values.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Audit comment lookup is not paginated</summary>

**Cause:** [`findSetupApplyComment`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b85f8323425354002a2bef7d96a5c375d3d412db/.agent/src/setup-apply.ts#L473) calls the issue comments API without `--paginate`.

**Candidate solutions:** Use `gh api --paginate` for marker lookup and add coverage for no-existing-comment and long-thread cases.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Deployment docs list the wrong permission for variable writes</summary>

**Cause:** The deployment docs say `/setup apply` repository variable updates belong under Actions write access.

**Candidate solutions:** Update both setup/app deployment docs to list repository `Variables: write` for variable create/update operations.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved findings are concrete branch changes in authorization, audit outcomes, parser validation, comment pagination, and docs permissions that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Remove `setup-apply` inheritance from `route_overrides.setup`, and update tests/docs for explicit apply-route override behavior.
- [ ] Record and report actual setup variable apply outcomes, including validation-blocked and mid-apply failure cases, with regression tests.
- [ ] Fail validation on missing or unknown required setup form choices instead of defaulting into a mutation plan.
- [ ] Paginate setup audit marker lookup and cover duplicate-prevention paths.
- [ ] Correct deployment docs to require repository `Variables: write` for `/setup apply` variable updates.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| review | fix-pr | PR #149 | 4 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: review verdict is needs_rework; dispatching fix-pr

- Record and report actual setup variable apply outcomes, including validation-blocked and mid-apply failure cases, with regression tests.
- Fail validation on missing or unknown required setup form choices instead of defaulting into a mutation plan.
- Paginate setup audit marker lookup and cover duplicate-prevention paths.
- Correct deployment docs to require repository `Variables: write` for `/setup apply` variable updates.

Constraints: Ignore optional INFO notes, metadata-only polish, already-fixed findings, and human-judgment nits unless required by those action items.

- Removed `setup-apply` inheritance from `route_overrides.setup`; apply access now requires `route_overrides.setup-apply` explicitly, with docs/tests updated.
- Setup apply now fails closed on missing or unknown required setup choices and reports actual audit outcomes for blocked validation and mid-apply failures.
- Paginated setup audit marker lookup and added duplicate-prevention coverage; deployment docs now require repository `Variables: write` for `/setup apply` variable updates.
- Verified with `npm --prefix .agent run build`, focused `node --test ...`, and `git diff --check`.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | `/setup apply` upserts one marked audit comment using `<!-- sepo-agent-setup-apply -->` and paginated lookup before posting. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The PR adds explicit `/setup apply` routing plus separate typed Project owner/title workflow inputs and setup issue fields rather than overloading one command string. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | Dry-run and apply share the same setup issue parsing, allowlisted plan construction, validation, and audit formatting; tests cover dry-run/blocked/error paths. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #149 implements child #148 under parent #126 after child #146/#147, preserving the staged boundary: deterministic variable apply only, no Project creation/linking or field sync. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to setup-apply routing, allowlisted variable application, assignment toggle/project context plumbing, docs, workflows, and focused tests required by #148. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `setup-apply` has its own privileged default access and no longer inherits `route_overrides.setup`; dispatch policy also requires explicit `/setup apply` and issue targets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR keeps workflow glue thin and puts mutation/audit behavior in typed `.agent/src` CLI/helper code, reusing existing GitHub helper patterns. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Access policy, supported workflows, quick start, project planning, configuration, and deployment docs now document `/setup apply`, explicit overrides, and `Variables: write`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Variable write failures are surfaced in audit output with `failed` / `not attempted` outcomes and nonzero CLI behavior; blocked validation reports no variables changed. |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | The audit comment uses a short status, concise error/notes sections, and a compact variable before/after/result table rather than raw logs. |

## Notes

- The fix-pr follow-up addressed the earlier rubric-relevant gaps: apply authorization no longer piggybacks on setup-plan overrides, audit rows now reflect actual outcomes, malformed setup choices fail closed, comment lookup is paginated, and deployment docs list `Variables: write`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. Remaining scope such as GitHub Project creation/linking and Project field sync is intentionally deferred by #148 and documented as out of scope.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #149 implements child issue #148 under parent #126 by adding deterministic `/setup apply` support after the setup-plan route. The route is issue-only, privileged, model-free, and limited to allowlisted repository variable create/update operations. It also wires assignment/project-management setup variables through workflows, updates docs, and adds focused tests for routing, validation, audit behavior, pagination, and permissions. The current follow-up fixed the earlier NEEDS_REWORK findings around route authorization, malformed form values, failed-write audit accuracy, pagination, and docs permissions.

## Review
Overall, the prior blocking findings appear resolved and the PR is close. Reviewers differed on final verdict: Claude and the rubrics review were ready to ship, while Codex found one remaining audit-comment ownership issue that should be fixed before merging.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Setup audit marker lookup trusts any commenter | WARNING | [`findSetupApplyComment`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3d4b14178c5666afaf8b54915625a74385e6f1c6/.agent/src/setup-apply.ts#L738) updates the first issue comment containing the hidden marker without checking that the comment was authored by the agent/app token. |

## Progress
- The earlier `setup-apply` access-override inheritance issue is fixed with a separate privileged route policy and tests.
- Setup form validation now fails closed for missing or unknown required choices.
- Apply audit rows now report blocked, failed, and not-attempted outcomes instead of only planned actions.
- Audit comment lookup is now paginated, and docs now mention `Variables: write`.
- Reviewers reported `.agent` build, focused tests, full tests, and `git diff --check` passing on PR head `3d4b14178c5666afaf8b54915625a74385e6f1c6`.
- A new inline comment was posted for the remaining marker-ownership issue.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Setup audit marker lookup trusts any commenter</summary>

**Cause:** `parseIssueComments` keeps only `id` and `body`, and `findSetupApplyComment` matches only `SETUP_APPLY_COMMENT_MARKER`. On public setup issues, a non-agent commenter could pre-seed the marker; `/setup apply` would then try to patch that comment or fail after variables were already changed.

**Candidate solutions:** Preserve comment author identity from the issue comments API and only update marker comments authored by the trusted Sepo app/bot identity. If the marker appears in an untrusted comment, ignore it and create a fresh agent-authored audit comment. Add a regression test for an untrusted marker comment followed by a trusted create path.

**Comments:** This does not undermine the allowlisted variable mutation path itself, but it weakens the privileged audit record and can turn a successful variable apply into a misleading or failed audit update.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: One concrete, localized branch change remains in setup audit comment lookup and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Filter setup audit marker matches to trusted agent/app-authored comments and add regression coverage for ignoring untrusted marker comments.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | Dry-run and apply use the same parsed intent, allowlisted plan, validation, and audit formatter; tests cover dry-run, blocked validation, and failed writes. |

## Notes

- The latest PR head addresses the earlier rubric-specific gaps: apply authorization is separate from plan authorization, malformed setup choices fail closed, audit rows reflect actual outcomes, marker lookup is paginated and trusted-author filtered, and docs list variable-write permissions.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. Project creation/linking and Project field sync remain intentionally out of scope for this staged child and are documented as deferred.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #149 implements child issue #148 under parent #126 for deterministic `/setup apply`.
- The route is explicit, issue-only, privileged, model-free, and limited to validated allowlisted repository variable updates.
- The PR also wires assignment/project-management setup variables, audit-comment upsert behavior, workflow routing, docs, and focused tests.
- GitHub Project creation/linking, Project fields/views, and Project field sync remain intentionally out of scope for this staged child.

## Review
All current reviewers are ship-positive after the latest fix-pr round. Codex and the rubrics review found no remaining issues; Claude flagged one optional user-facing wording polish, which is line-specific but not behaviorally risky.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Optional setup-audit wording polish | INFO | A setup audit warning says “in this child,” which exposes internal orchestration language to repository owners. This is cosmetic and does not affect authorization, mutation safety, or audit correctness. |

## Progress
- Earlier findings around `setup-apply` authorization, failed-write audit accuracy, malformed setup form handling, comment pagination, docs permissions, and trusted marker ownership are fixed.
- Current reviewer outputs report the latest head `44ef289d9addec4ec922bd8aed650b9cc31bfc71` as otherwise clean.
- A concise inline comment was posted for the optional wording polish at `.agent/src/setup-apply.ts:489`.
- Project creation/linking and Project field sync are acknowledged as deferred scope.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Optional setup-audit wording polish</summary>

**Cause:** [`buildSetupVariablePlan`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/44ef289d9addec4ec922bd8aed650b9cc31bfc71/.agent/src/setup-apply.ts#L489) emits a user-visible audit note with the phrase “in this child.”

**Candidate solutions:** Rephrase the warning in repository-owner terms, such as saying Project creation is not performed by `/setup apply` and should be configured separately after setup variables are applied.

**Comments:** Claude labeled this as a warning; Codex and the rubrics review had no findings. Since the concern is wording-only and all reviewers still recommend shipping, this synthesis treats it as optional INFO.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
HUMAN_DECISION: The only remaining note is optional user-facing copy polish, so maintainers can choose whether to merge as-is or make a small wording change without another automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch changes.

title: Add deterministic setup apply route
